# OIP — Open Ingestion Protocol

> A vendor-neutral spec for tools that ingest source material (PDFs, audio,
> code, web) and produce structured, source-grounded knowledge any
> OIP-aware consumer can read. v0.1 (draft).

## What this is

OIP is a small contract a producer satisfies to be discoverable + readable
by any OIP consumer. Producers don't import consumers; consumers don't
import producers; they meet at a documented on-disk layout + a manifest
+ a small MCP tool surface.

## For LLM agents implementing an OIP producer

Read these files in order:

1. [SPEC.md](./SPEC.md) — full protocol (RFC-style, ~250 lines). MUST/SHOULD/MAY conformance.
2. [CHECKLIST.md](./CHECKLIST.md) — go/no-go acceptance list for an implementation.
3. [examples/transcriber.json](./examples/transcriber.json) — complete worked example.
4. [schemas/manifest.json](./schemas/manifest.json), [schemas/document.json](./schemas/document.json), [schemas/region.json](./schemas/region.json) — JSON Schemas for validation.

## Tooling

```
uvx oip-for-agents spec            → full SPEC.md
uvx oip-for-agents validate <dir>  → check a producer's output
uvx oip-for-agents new <name>      → scaffold a producer starter
```

## Reference implementations

- Producer: PDF medallion pipeline + FMU inspector — github.com/Novia-RDI-Seafaring/anchor-kb-ui-RAG (v2/)
- Consumer: Anchor canvas — same repo

## Conformance summary (the SHOULD-cares)

- Producer writes `manifest.json` at root, `artefacts/<slug>/{document.json,regions.json,content/}` per ingested doc.
- Manifest declares `source_kinds`, `region_kinds`, `source_ref_kinds`, and an `mcp-stdio` invocation with a `tools_namespace`.
- Producer's MCP server exposes `<ns>.{ingest,list_documents,get_document,get_regions,get_region_content}`.
- Producer's installer drops the manifest into `${XDG_CONFIG_HOME:-~/.config}/oip/producers.d/<name>.json`.
- Source-ref kinds are namespaced when domain-specific (`mytool-frame`, not `frame`).

That's the whole contract. Read SPEC.md for the rules.
